title: Argentina vs Austria LIVE: World Cup 2026 result, latest updates and reaction | The Standard
original_text:
Argentina vs Austria LIVE: World Cup 2026 result, latest updates and reaction
Live: Argentina vs Austria
Getty
Lionel Messi wrote himself further into footballing history as he became the all-time leading World Cup goalscorer during Argentina’s 2-0 win over Austria in Arlington, Texas. The 39-year-old was just one goal away from surpassing the great Miroslav Klose, and the Inter Miami forward notched both goals at the AT&T Stadium to smash the record as the holders topped Group J.
He was handed an early opportunity to break the record from the spot as Lautaro Martinez was fouled by Stefan Posch, in a decision overturned by VAR. Messi uncharactersitically missed from 12 yards, slotting wide of the post, but he had his moment later in the first half, sweeping home past Alexander Schlager after a flowing Argentine move.
He doubled his tally in second-half stoppage time, persisting after being denied not once but twice by a resolute Austrian defence, as he forced home the goal that killed an otherwise lacklustre game. Lionel Scaloni’s holders top Group J, and will progress into the knockout rounds as group winners. Messi has scored all five of his country’s goals in this summer’s competition.
Argentina vs Austria latest updates
Live updates
1 day ago
FT: Argentina 2-0 Austria
There we have it.
Argentina top Group J, and are two wins from two.
Thanks for joining us on Standard Sport, be sure to come back soon for even more LIVE action.
1 day ago
Goal! Argentina 2-0 Austria
90+5 mins: It’s two at the death, and Messi has his second.
18 World Cup goals for the greatest to ever do it, as he is denied not once but twice, but he doesn’t give up, and he forces it home at the third time of asking!
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
90+2 mins: Time is ticking away and Argentina top the group as it stands. Messi’s goal the difference.
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
90 mins: Five added minutes.
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
88 mins: Chance! Gonzalez breaks clear and has a chance to seal it, but he’s thwarted at the last by Schlager!
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
86 mins: Former Chelsea midfielder Carney Chukwuemeka is on, replacing Gregoritsch.
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
83 mins: Final knockings of this game. It’s not been the thriller that the neutrals had hoped for. Can the Austrians find an equaliser?
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
80 mins: Wimmer replaces Schmid.
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
78 mins: Yellow cards apiece for Medina and Laimer after a coming together between the two. A push from Laimer, and some afters between the pair.
1 day ago
Argentina 1-0 Austria
75 mins: 15 minutes to play. Can the holders kill this game off?
